channel.title: # 🇱🇰 Sri Lanka This Week
channel.description: Generated by gpt-5 from 98 English News Articles published between 2026-04-08 and 2026-04-14.

item.title: IMF staff-level deal clears $700m tranche for Sri Lanka
item.description: Sri Lanka reached a staff-level agreement with the International Monetary Fund on the combined Fifth and Sixth reviews of its four-year Extended Fund Facility, paving the way—subject to Board approval—for access to about $700 million. Upon disbursement, total IMF support under the programme would reach roughly $2.4 billion, with authorities reporting low-single-digit inflation and higher reserves in early 2026. 💰

item.title: Government launches Rs. 2.16bn upgrade of Colombo Fort station
item.description: The Transport, Highways and Urban Development Ministry kicked off a 15‑month modernisation of Colombo Fort Railway Station, financed by the Asian Development Bank and the Government at a total cost exceeding Rs. 2.16 billion. The project includes automated ticketing, lifts, overhead bridges, new buildings to enhance passenger safety, and upgraded sanitation—aimed at improving services at the country’s busiest rail hub. 🛠️

item.title: Regulators impose curbs as NDB flags Rs. 13.2bn fraud
item.description: Following disclosures by National Development Bank PLC of a fraud estimated at Rs. 13.2 billion, regulatory authorities ordered the suspension of cash dividends scheduled for April 2026, limits on discretionary spending, and a halt to branch expansion. The bank has moved to appoint an independent forensic auditor, while parliamentary committees initiated scrutiny of control and oversight practices across the sector.

item.title: Northern Line rail services resume after cyclone damage
item.description: Train operations on the Northern Railway Line restarted after restoration of cyclone‑affected sections under an India-supported grant of $5 million, part of a wider $450 million package. The works were completed ahead of the Sinhala and Tamil New Year, reinstating a key inter-provincial transport link. 🚆

item.title: Colombo Dockyard installs new board after MDL takes 51% stake
item.description: Colombo Dockyard PLC announced board changes effective 7 April 2026, with Capt. (Retd.) Jagmohan appointed Non‑Executive Chairman. India’s Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders Ltd., now holding a 51% majority stake, nominated Thimira S. Godakumbura as Managing Director/CEO, and added Vish Govindasamy and Senthilverl Nandhanan as non‑executive nominee directors, aligning governance with the new ownership structure. ⚓

item.title: Motor Traffic chief further remanded in vehicle registration case
item.description: The Pilessa Magistrate’s Court ordered the further remand, until 20 April, of Motor Traffic Department Commissioner General Kamal Amarasinghe, who was arrested on 27 March by the Criminal Investigation Department. Police said charges relate to aiding and abetting the fraudulent registration of a vehicle by altering a chassis number. ⚖️
